Article Title: How to get your CIBIL credit score without a PAN card
Article Text:
Your credit score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity.

What is a CIBIL score?

How to get your CIBIL Credit score without a PAN Card

Factors affecting your CIBIL score

CIBIL credit score : Understanding your creditworthiness is pivotal in today's financial landscape, especially when seeking loans or credit cards. The Credit Information Bureau (India) Limited, commonly known as CIBIL, plays an important role in this regard by providing credit scores to borrowers.According to ET, credit score helps banks make informed decisions about extending credit cards and loans. Your credit history influences your CIBIL score , which is calculated using a special method.Your CIBIL score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity. Ranging from 300 to 900, a higher score indicates a stronger likelihood of loan approval. Banks and financial institutions use this score to assess the risk associated with lending money.1. Go to the official CIBIL website and click on 'Personal CIBIL score,' then 'Get Your Free CIBIL score.' Follow the link provided and fill in all the required details.2. If you don’t have a PAN Card, you can use an alternative identification such as Passport, Voter ID, Driver’s License, or Ration Card. Enter the number of the chosen ID.3. Provide your date of birth, PIN code, and select your state. Finally, enter your mobile number and click on ‘Accept and Continue’.4. Authenticate your identity by entering the OTP (One-Time Password) delivered to your mobile device on the next screen. Click 'Continue' to proceed.5. Choose whether you want to link the device with your account. Select 'Yes' or 'No' based on your preference.6. Upon successful registration, you will see a page confirming that 'You have successfully registered!'7. Click on ‘Go to Dashboard’ to access and view your CIBIL score.The factors influencing the new CIBIL score, as stated on the CIBIL website, include:1. Depth of credit: This refers to the duration of your existing credit history, calculated from the opening date of your oldest credit account.2. Long-term trend of outstanding balances: The overall trend of outstanding balances over time affects your score.3. Transaction history on credit cards: How you manage transactions on your credit cards impacts your score.4. Ratio of actual repayment amount to total amount due: This ratio indicates your repayment behavior and influences your score accordingly.5. Number of new accounts opened and number of accounts closed: Opening or closing accounts can affect your score, so managing these actions wisely is important.
Article Summary: Your CIBIL score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity. Ranging from 300 to 900, a higher score indicates a stronger likelihood of loan approval. Banks and financial institutions use this score to assess the risk associated with lending money.

Article Title: Sublime Lands at BLR Airport T2, Elevating Gifting in the Skies with Curated Luxury
Article Text:
Disclaimer - The above content is non-editorial, and TIL hereby disclaims any and all warranties, expressed or implied, relating to it, and does not guarantee, vouch for or necessarily endorse any of the content.

As travel retail in India moves beyond duty-free staples toward curated, gift-worthy experiences, homegrown luxury brands are finding a natural home in the country's busiest terminals. Tapping into this shift is Sublime, a luxury brand offering a thoughtfully curated range of tea, dry fruits, spices, and honey sourced from across India, which has opened its first airport store at Terminal 2 of Kempegowda International Airport Bengaluru (BLR Airport), marking its entry into travel retail with a distinctive focus on curated gifting and elegant crockery that offers travellers a seamless blend of sophistication and convenience.Strategically located within the iconic Terminal 2, the Sublime store is designed for discerning travellers seeking meaningful purchases on the go. The space showcases a thoughtfully curated selection of teas, alongside an extensive range of gift options. Each gift set is carefully assembled to reflect balance, attention to detail, and refined presentation, making it ideal for both personal gestures and corporate giftingA defining feature of the store is its exclusive crockery units, which elevate the gifting experience. Designed for timeless appeal, these pieces complement Sublime’s curated collections and turn each gift into a lasting keepsake. Integrating crockery with the brand’s offerings ensures that every purchase offers both aesthetic and functional value.Speaking at the launch, Uzma Irfan, Founder of Sublime and Whole Time Director of Prestige Group, said, “The opening of Sublime at BLR Airport’s Terminal 2 marks a significant step as we expand into travel retail. Today’s travellers are seeking thoughtful, well-presented gifting solutions that reflect both quality and intention. With our curated collection of various products and signature crockery, we aim to offer a refined experience that resonates even amid the pace of travel. ”Designed to align with Terminal 2’s nature-inspired architecture, the store offers a warm and inviting ambience that reflects Sublime’s philosophy of thoughtful living and elevated design. The space encourages travellers to pause, explore, and engage with products that are both purposeful and beautifully presented. With this launch, Sublime strengthens its presence in experiential retail by bringing together luxury, curation, and design-led gifting at one of India’s busiest travel destinations.
Article Summary: Luxury brand Sublime has opened its first airport store at Terminal 2 of Kempegowda International Airport Bengaluru (BLR Airport) The store is designed for discerning travellers seeking meaningful purchases on the go. The space showcases a thoughtfully curated selection of teas, alongside an extensive range of gift options.

Article Title: Researchers just found that job seekers may be better off replying fast, because an analysis of 11.6 million marketplace interactions suggests slower responses hurt hiring chances and quick replies ma
Article Text:
In today's job market, especially for freelancers, how quickly you respond to inquiries can significantly impact your chances of getting hired. Image Credit: ChatGPT

Speed Is a Signal: When

Faster Replies

Increase Hiring Likelihood

The hidden psychology of instant

digital communication

New research reveals that employers form quick impressions based on reply times, often interpreting faster responses as enthusiasm and reliability. This study highlights that even small delays can cost you opportunities, making prompt communication a crucial skill for professional success in the modern workplace. Image Credit: ChatGPT

Getting an undeniable advantage in the contemporary hybrid workplace

When we look at the small anxieties built into modern technology, few things are as tense as the text bubble. We watch those three little dots dance on our screens, or we glance at our phones, hoping for a notification banner to break a long, quiet afternoon silence. In personal life, people often read response times as a sign of anything from busyness to distance. For a long time, we treated these digital habits as simple, casual quirks of our social lives. But new tracking data shows that even small delays can matter when you are trying to land a job.Imagine submitting a spectacular portfolio or matching perfectly with a freelance listing online, only to have the entire opportunity slip away simply because you decided to wait until after dinner to reply to an initial inquiry. In the modern, always-connected freelance market, your professional destiny is often decided long before you ever jump on an introductory phone call or sit down for a formal video panel. A study found that employers make quick assumptions about a candidate's personality traits based on how long they take to reply.This profound behavioural pattern was detailed in a research paper published in the journal Management Science titled. The study utilised transaction data from approximately eleven point six million real-world marketplace interactions on a massive global freelancing portal.The data showed that longer reply delays were linked to a lower chance of getting the contract, making response speed an important competitive factor.To comprehend how the quick message influences contemporary recruitment managers, one should consider the psychological factors that exist beyond the screen. When the client contacts the freelancer or a potential worker, they do not only assess their technical competence, past projects, or star rating. What they need is to find the intangible level of professional security and trust between both parties. According to the results obtained during several experiment stages, a quick answer could be interpreted as a sign of enthusiasm and hard work.The behavioural trials suggest that replying quickly can change how an evaluator judges a candidate's character. Candidates who managed to reply within a tight window gave more positive impressions to the hiring managers than their slower counterparts. It also signalled future responsiveness, making employers more likely to see the candidate as organised and reliable.This study reveals the possibility that the halo effect could operate regardless of the message content or past quality metrics. Sometimes, even if the slow responder received better reviews or had a lower price, he still managed to win the contract. Reading a late message is likely to be taken as a signal of indifference or lack of organisation, which makes it harder to contact the slower employee under deadlines.The practical insights gained from this large-scale market research data provide the key tool for all those who are working in the competitive world of freelance and remote recruitment. In the world of hybrid workforces and teams around the globe, being reachable becomes a valuable professional skill that can be used to enhance one’s market value without any additional certification.By setting up notifications, keeping templates ready, and replying promptly during hiring windows, independent professionals can signal professionalism to potential buyers. This proactive approach helps build an immediate sense of social comfort and trust with a potential client before any physical work even begins. It can turn a digital exchange into a more personal interaction and give fast responders an advantage over slower competitors.The results of the research suggest that reaction time could be regarded as a signal. Fast reactions of the respondents will help to reassure the client, establish one’s reputation and increase one’s opportunities of being hired. Workers may demonstrate their professionalism by fast reactions.
Article Summary: How quickly you respond to inquiries can significantly impact your chances of getting hired. A study found that employers make quick assumptions about a candidate's personality traits based on how long they take to reply. Candidates who managed to reply within a tight window gave more positive impressions to the hiring managers than their slower counterparts.

Article Title: Sold a company at 16, raised $3 million at 19, received US O-1 visa: How Dhravya Shah built AI startup Supermemory
Article Text:
From dorm room projects to Supermemory



Dhravya Shah secured $3 million seed funding



Walking away from IIT



Recognition and U.S. O-1 visa



Why Supermemory matters



Mumbai-born entrepreneur Dhravya Shah built his own-company Supermemory, an AI startup that raised $3 million in seed funding. Shah also managed to secure a US O-1 visa. Shah’s entrepreneurial journey started from selling his first company at 16 to walking away from the IIT dream. His journey also highlights how a solo founder turned a dorm room project into a critical infrastructure for AI products. Supermemory began as a hackathon project in 2024 — a consumer “second brain” app that rocketed up the GitHub charts, surpassing 10k stars and becoming one of the fastest-growing OSS projects of the year.“Not long after launching the consumer version of Supermemory, Dhravya started getting inbound from other startups asking for help setting up memory systems for their own products. When Dhravya started pulling the thread, he saw that if he really wanted to have an impact improving memory for AI he would need to start serving other developers. He’s been pursuing it ever since,” reads Solofounders blog.Before starting Supermemory, Shah shipped more than 15 open-source tools from his college dorm, never charging users. One of these a personal context collector gained traction and evolved into Supermemory.His approach has been marked by constant pivots, moving from a consumer “second brain” to the universal memory API that now powers AI applications.In October 2025, Shah raised $3 million (TechCrunch confirmed $2.6M, while Indian outlets reported $3M) in seed funding for Supermemory. The round was led by Susa Ventures, Browder Capital, and SF1.vc, with backing from industry leaders including Google AI chief Jeff Dean, Cloudflare CTO Dane Knecht, DeepMind PM Logan Kilpatrick, and Sentry founder David Cramer. Supermemory’s technology extracts structured “memories” from unstructured data—files, chats, emails—and builds a knowledge graph to surface context for LLM-based apps. Early customers included Cluely, Montra, and SciraShah’s path was unconventional. He abandoned his IIT aspirations to pursue entrepreneurship, a decision that led him to build Supermemory at 19. His story has been widely covered in Indian media as an example of how young founders are reshaping the AI landscape.Shah’s achievements earned him the U.S. O-1 visa, reserved for individuals with extraordinary ability. This recognition underscores his growing influence in the AI ecosystem and validates Supermemory’s role as a foundational layer for AI products.Persistent memory remains a major challenge for AI systems, which often lose context across sessions. Supermemory addresses this gap by enabling multi-session coherence and faster retrieval than retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) alternatives. Its differentiation lies in lower latency and robust access-control design, making it a promising infrastructure tool for developers.
Article Summary: Dhravya Shah built Supermemory, an AI startup that raised $3 million in seed funding. Shah also managed to secure a US O-1 visa. Shah’s entrepreneurial journey started from selling his first company at 16 to walking away from the IIT dream.

Article Title: YouTube Shorts now play at double speed with one tap
Article Text:
YouTube Shorts got a speed button you'll actually use

What else is new on Shorts?

YouTube Shorts now plays at double speed. Of the four changes YouTube began rolling out on June 25, this is the one viewers actually asked for—the company says speed ranked among the most requested Shorts features.It works the way long-form viewers already expect. Tap and hold on a Short, then drag down to lock 2x for the rest of the clip. The payoff shows up most on Shorts that creep toward the three-minute mark, where you can blow past the slow bits or jump to the part that matters. People have done this with podcasts and tutorials for years. Now the habit reaches short-form too.The rest are quieter tweaks. Clear Screen mode strips away every overlay for an unobstructed view, a trick TikTok pulled first. Muting takes fewer taps now: pause the video, then hit the mute icon. The thorniest change is the missing dislike button, with YouTube nudging viewers toward "Not Interested" and "Don't recommend this channel," and turning the thumbs-up into a heart.Most of this already lives on Instagram Reels, so YouTube is playing catch-up more than setting the pace. Then again, Shorts pulled 200 billion daily views last year, so it can afford to follow. The rollout is gradual, which means 2x might not hit your feed for a while.
Article Summary: YouTube Shorts now plays at double speed. Of the four changes YouTube began rolling out on June 25, this is the one viewers actually asked for. The rollout is gradual, which means 2x might not hit your feed for a while.

Article Title: Italy launches probe into Microsoft over automatic upgrade to costlier AI subscriptions
Article Text:
Representative Image

Microsoft says it will cooperate



Italy’s antitrust watchdog has announced an official investigation into tech giant Microsoft over alleged unfair and aggressive commercial practices regarding the price increases for its popular “Microsoft 365” subscription service. According to a report by news agency Reuters, the Italian Competition Authority stated that the software company failed to properly inform everyday consumers that it was baking its new artificial intelligence (AI) tools, “Copilot” and “Designer,” into the standard Microsoft 365 package, and passing the bill onto the users.The regulatory watchdog says that Microsoft automatically migrated existing customers into a more expensive subscription tier that included the new AI features. If users did not want the upgrade or the higher fee, they had to dig through their settings to opt out, it added, as per Reuters.The regulator criticised this approach, stating that Microsoft provided “insufficient information” for consumers to make a decision on whether to renew their contracts or accept the new charges.By using this tactics of pushing users onto a pricier plan by default, the watchdog warned that Microsoft's tactics could legally be classified as ‘aggressive’ because they unfairly restricted a consumer’s freedom of choice.The cloud and software giant relies heavily on its subscription models for global revenue, and integrating AI into its core office products has been a centerpiece of its business strategy. However, European regulators are increasingly cracking down on tech companies that use “dark patterns”.Microsoft has signaled it will work alongside local officials to resolve the dispute.“Microsoft is committed to complying with Italian consumer law and will cooperate with the Italian Competition Authority in its preliminary investigation,” a Microsoft company spokesperson was quoted as saying.
Article Summary: Italy's antitrust watchdog has announced an official investigation into tech giant Microsoft over alleged unfair and aggressive commercial practices. The software company failed to properly inform everyday consumers that it was baking its new artificial intelligence (AI) tools into the standard Microsoft 365 package. Microsoft has signaled it will work alongside local officials to resolve the dispute.

You can consider buying Sony 139 cm (55 inches) BRAVIA 3II Ultra HD Smart LED Google TV, Samsung 55 inches Mini LED 4K Samsung Vision AI Smart TV.* HP OmniBook 3 Core Ultra 9: AI-enhanced productivity laptop.* Garmin Forerunner 170: Advanced health tracking with GPS.Other launches include Lenovo Tab Plus Gen-2, JBL Live 780NC headphones, BenQ and Lifelong projectors, and TCL Smart TVs.* Rufus AI Assistant: Personalized recommendations, price insights, and product comparisons.* Lens AI: Visual search for products.* AI Review Highlights & Quick View: Summarized insights to simplify decision-making.* Prime Playback: An AI-powered recap of each member’s Prime journey.To celebrate a decade of Prime in India, Amazon Pay is offering its most rewarding program yet:* 10% cashback with Amazon Pay ICICI Bank Credit Card and Amazon Pay Later.* Unlimited 5% cashback for ICICI Bank cardholders.* Three-month No Cost EMI with zero interest and processing fees.

Article Title: Envent Music Lab launched to promote India’s new music talent: All detail
Article Text:
Envent Music Lab

What Envent Music Lab offers

Rohini Ahluwalia, COO of Envent The House Brands

“India has a lot of talent that is yet to be discovered. Envent Music Lab is our effort to give artists a platform, visibility, and support.

We want to create opportunities where passion meets the audience and talent gets the recognition it deserves.”

"🎤 The stage is set. The spotlight is waiting. 🙏 Welcome to Envent Music Lab — where raw talent meets real opportunities. Get ready to showcase your passion, connect with audiences, and take your music to the next level. 🚀 Your sound. Your story. Your moment."

Envent has launched, a new platform aimed at discovering and supporting emerging music talent across India. The initiative is open to singers, musicians, DJs, dancers and other performers, giving them a space to showcase their work, build an audience and participate in live and digital events.The company said creators will be able to upload short-form videos through the platform. Artists whose content receives significant engagement will receive support from the Envent team while retaining ownership and intellectual property rights in their work.Envent will launch a YouTube channel for this initiative that will feature performances by Music Lab artists. Envent will also give out monthly awards and recognition to performers based on their performances.In addition, Envent said it intends to organise concerts and digital events featuring selected artists from the Music Lab community, providing them with opportunities to perform before larger audiences.Explaining the objective behind the platform,, said,According to the company, Envent Music Lab is now accepting entries from artists looking to showcase their talent and grow their audience. The platform is designed to encourage creators to produce original content while allowing them to retain ownership of their work.Announcing the launch on Instagram, Envent The House Brands posted:With the launch of Envent Music Lab, the company is bringing together talent discovery, digital content, artist promotion, and live performances on a single platform to help emerging performers connect with wider audiences.
Article Summary: Envent has launched, a new platform aimed at discovering and supporting emerging music talent across India. The initiative is open to singers, musicians, DJs, dancers and other performers, giving them a space to showcase their work, build an audience and participate in live and digital events.
